summary: Jeff Atwood shares a quick syntax highlighting tool called Squishy Syntax Highlighter and demonstrates it with a VB.NET CRC32 implementation he had previously found. He notes that Visual Studio automatically preserves syntax highlighting when pasting into RtfTextBox controls, which sparks an idea about converting RTF to HTML instead of using hardcoded highlighter settings. The post is primarily a code snippet share with a brief musing about workflow optimization. It represents the early, more utilitarian nature of Coding Horror before the blog became known for longer-form essays.
key_points: Squishy Syntax Highlighter provides quick HTML syntax highlighting for code snippets
Visual Studio preserves IDE syntax highlighting when pasting into RTF controls
Converting RTF to HTML could preserve personal IDE highlighting preferences instead of using a tool's hardcoded colors
The CRC32 implementation uses a standard lookup table approach for calculating 32-bit checksums
Early blog posts often served as personal code snippet repositories
money_quotes: I wonder if it might be easier to just convert the RTF to HTML – at least that way, I get my IDE highlighting settings instead of the hard coded Squishy settings.
key_insight: Developer tools that preserve your personal workflow preferences (like IDE color schemes) are worth building, even if alternatives exist.
